message:
perf(connectors): replace per-file canonicalize() with symlink-only deduplication in scan hot paths

Every connector's scan loop previously called std::fs::canonicalize() on
every discovered session file to build deduplication keys. canonicalize()
walks every path component issuing readlink syscalls, which dominates
wall-clock time during no-op incremental scans when thousands of session
files exist but none have changed.

The vast majority of indexed session files are regular files, not symlinks.
The new dedupe_path_key() in utils.rs checks symlink_metadata() first (a
single lstat) and only calls canonicalize() when the leaf is actually a
symlink. Regular files use the original path directly as the dedupe key.

Changed connectors:
- codex.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in session scan
- factory.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in generic scan
- goose.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in JSONL file scan
- kimi.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in wire file scan
- opencode.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in session scan
- pi_agent.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in session scan
- qwen.rs: replace canonicalize() with dedupe_path_key() in session scan

New utility (utils.rs):
- dedupe_path_key(): checks symlink_metadata() to detect symlinks, only
  calling canonicalize() for actual symlinks; returns path as-is for regular
  files — eliminating the readlink storm on every scan iteration
- Tests: regular file path stability, symlink leaf canonicalization (unix)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
